opp.name: Quest Diagnostics [S2P] Jan'26
opp.next_step: Paul will get back in a week about the status of the project. He liked Merlin and it's use cases, they do not have any centralized system at the moment.
opp.ais_why:
• Next step states verbatim: "He liked Merlin and it's use cases, they do not have any centralized system at the moment" — explicit named-product pull from Paul.
• Full S2P opp with no centralized system in place — greenfield where Merlin and Agentic AI can lead, and Paul is the named champion endorsing the use cases.
• No Avoma calls on file, so confidence is Medium. The recency-weighted Merlin endorsement (last activity 4/24) anchors the AI Hungry call.

items.quote: No EB Contact Role, no X10a_Sponsor__c or Who_will_approve_budget__c populated; commercial authority unmapped.
What_is_the_decision_process__c, Mandate__c, approval fields empty; no insight into RFP/RFI gates or committee structure.
Competitors__c null, no rival mentioned in NextStep or tasks, zero Avoma calls; unknown if sole-source or multi-vendor evaluation.
items.source: Salesforce Q3, NextStep 12 Jun, Avoma discovery (zero calls)

items.quote: No quantified pain, ROI, or savings documented; qualitative pain (no centralized system) confirmed but not costed.
items.source: NextStep 12 Jun, Salesforce Q2 (no metrics fields)

items.quote: No RFP, BRD, or evaluation scorecard shared; fit against weighted criteria unconfirmed.
Only 2 buyer contact roles (Paul Butts, Kathryn Schneider); no EB, DM, finance, IT, or InfoSec contacts on the opp.

sources: Salesforce Q2/Q3 field reads (no metrics fields populated)
metrics.narrative: No quantified business problem or financial impact documented in Salesforce or available call transcripts. NextStep (12 Jun) notes they lack a centralized system, implying manual/fragmented procurement processes, but Paul did not articulate cost, cycle time, error rate, headcount burden, or ROI expectations. The pain is qualitative (operational inefficiency from decentralization) but never quantified. Customer_Business_Problem__c, Business_Objectives__c, Value_to_Customer__c, and Pain_points_in_the_current_solution__c fields are absent from this org or unpopulated. Without a quantified value case, building the business case for Merlin and justifying the investment to the Economic Buyer and CFO will be difficult. The immediate move is to run a discovery session with Paul (and ideally a finance or ops stakeholder) to quantify the current-state cost of decentralized intake and manual sourcing, then model the savings Merlin Intake would deliver.

sources: Salesforce LastActivityDate 12 Jun, now 14 days ago with no reply
champion.narrative: Paul Butts (Director, Corporate Procurement Business Transformation) is a developing champion but not yet strong. Evidence of advocacy: Paul engaged on 12 Jun and expressed positive sentiment toward Merlin and its use cases, a clear signal of personal interest. He holds a transformation-focused role (Business Transformation) and is the primary contact, suggesting influence over procurement modernization initiatives. However, his advocacy is incomplete: he has not introduced us to the Economic Buyer, Decision Maker, or other committee members; he has not shared internal documents (BRD, RFP, evaluation criteria); and he has not actively pushed back on competitors or driven the agenda forward (his commitment to reply in one week has now gone silent for 7 days past the expected 19 Jun date). Current engagement is cooling—no reply after the promised one-week check-in. Paul's access to power is unconfirmed (we do not know if he reports to or can influence the EB/CFO). The champion is developing but at risk of going passive. The immediate move is to re-engage Paul with a value-add (e.g. a Merlin ROI one-pager, a peer healthcare reference), reconfirm his internal timeline, and request an introduction to the budget authority and any other evaluation committee members.

competition.narrative: Competition is unknown. Competitors__c is unpopulated, and no competitive intel appears in Salesforce free-text fields (How_are_you_addressing_your_problem_toda__c, Existing_vendor__c, Replacing_What__c, Others_Competitors_Please_specify__c, Why_not_Zycus__c, Zycus_Differentiation_Why_Zycus__c), NextStep, or completed Tasks. Zero Avoma buyer calls were discovered via opp/account/attendee pulls, so no transcripts exist to reveal competitive shortlists, vendor comparisons, or incumbent mentions. NextStep (12 Jun) states they have no centralized system, which could mean no incumbent (greenfield opportunity, favoring Zycus as a sole-source or first-mover) or could mean they are solving the problem manually today and evaluating multiple vendors to centralize (competitive bake-off underway but not disclosed to us). The knowledge gap is genuine and high-risk: without knowing the competitive landscape, we cannot differentiate, tailor messaging, or forecast win probability. The next move is to ask Paul directly in the re-engagement call: 'Who else are you looking at for intake automation?' and 'How are you solving this today?', then adjust positioning based on his answer.

identify_pain.narrative: Pain is confirmed but not owned or quantified. NextStep (12 Jun) states Quest 'do not have any centralized system at the moment,' implying fragmented, manual procurement intake and sourcing processes across a large healthcare/diagnostics enterprise. This is operational pain (decentralized intake creates inefficiency, lack of visibility, slow cycle times, and likely maverick spend), but Paul did not name the business owner of that pain (a VP of Procurement, a CFO cost-reduction mandate, an ops leader), nor did he quantify the impact (hours spent, error rate, cost per requisition, headcount burden). The pain is real and fits Merlin Intake's AI automation value prop, but without a named pain owner and quantified current-state cost, we cannot build a compelling ROI case or map to a forcing function. The next move is to ask Paul who internally feels this pain most acutely and what the decentralization is costing them (time, dollars, operational risk), then validate that with the pain owner directly.

paper_process.narrative: Paper process and contracting mechanics are unknown. No MSA, SOW, legal review, procurement channel, InfoSec questionnaire, or SI/partner involvement has been discussed. Current_Contract_Expiration__c is absent or empty (no incumbent contract forcing function identified). We do not know whether Quest requires InfoSec review (likely for a SaaS intake tool touching supplier data), whether they have a standard MSA or will redline ours, or how long their legal/procurement cycle runs. Given the October close date (119 days out) and the deal still at Qualified with no demo or RFP, legal and InfoSec steps are not yet on the calendar but will consume weeks once pricing is agreed. The gap is genuine. The move is to surface contracting and InfoSec early in the next conversation—ask Paul whether Quest has standard terms, who runs InfoSec reviews, and what lead time legal requires—so we can build those gates into the mutual close plan.

sources: Account contacts (finance execs present but not linked to this opp)
economic_buyer.narrative: Economic Buyer is not identified. No EB Contact Role exists, and no Salesforce fields (X10a_Sponsor__c, Who_will_approve_budget__c, Executive_Sponsor_Identified__c) name a budget authority. The two known contacts are Paul Butts (Director, Corporate Procurement Business Transformation) and Kathryn Schneider (Strategic Sourcing Manager); both are influencers/users but neither holds CFO or VP-level commercial authority for a $110k SaaS decision. The account-level contact list includes finance executives (Laura Bloomstein, Finance Director; Cari Borgna, VP Chief Tax and Finance Transformation Officer; multiple Region CFOs) who may control budget, but none are confirmed involved in this opportunity. No Avoma calls were discovered to reveal who drove pricing or commercial conversations. The gap is genuine and blocking: without mapping the EB, we cannot validate budget availability, forecast timing, or build exec sponsorship. The next move is for Edward to ask Paul directly who will approve the Merlin budget and commercial terms, then secure an introduction.

decision_process.narrative: Decision process is unknown. What_is_the_decision_process__c, Mandate__c, Does_the_Buyer_need_approval__c, Purchase_approvals_Required_from__c, and Multiple_approvals__c fields are absent or empty. The call sequence itself is invisible (zero buyer calls discovered). We do not know whether Quest runs a formal RFP/RFI, how many vendors they are evaluating, who joins at each stage (procurement, finance, IT, legal, InfoSec), or what the approval chain looks like for a $110k SaaS purchase. Paul's 12 Jun comment that he will report back on 'the status of the project' in one week implies internal review or committee discussion is underway, but we were not told the structure, timeline, or gates. The gap blocks our ability to plan milestones and forecast timing. The next move is to ask Paul (when he replies or when we re-engage) to walk us through their vendor selection process, approval steps, and internal timeline, then map it to our October close date.

sources: No Avoma calls with use-case validation or BRD review
decision_criteria.narrative: Decision criteria are not documented. No RFP, RFI, BRD, or evaluation scorecard has been shared. Business_Requirements__c, Top_Challenges_Priorities__c, Decision_Maker_Identified__c, and Shoe_Fit_Criteria_Met__c fields are absent or empty. NextStep (12 Jun) confirms Paul liked Merlin and its use cases, suggesting AI-driven intake automation resonated, but we do not know the weighted criteria Quest will use to compare vendors (e.g. AI/ML capability, integration with their ERP/systems, implementation timeline, cost per transaction, supplier network coverage, or InfoSec/compliance requirements). Without criteria, we cannot tailor the demo, differentiate against competitors, or prove fit against their BRD. The knowledge gap is genuine. The move is to request their evaluation framework or RFP requirements in the next conversation, or if none exists, co-create a criteria checklist with Paul anchored to their pain (decentralization, manual processes) and Merlin's AI strengths.

cro_panel.intro: One read per score, in language a CRO/CO can act on. We're behind on this one — the reads below say why and what would change it.

blocks.read: We're well behind — this one is cold.

ai_fit_signal.summary: AIS fields are absent or unpopulated, but NextStep (12 Jun) provides a direct AI appetite signal: Paul 'liked Merlin and it's use cases,' confirming interest in AI-driven intake automation. Quest lacks a centralized system, making them a fit for Merlin's AI orchestration (automated requisition routing, supplier matching, intelligent intake workflows). The signal is AI Curious leaning toward AI Hungry—Paul's transformation-focused role and positive response to Merlin's AI capabilities suggest openness to AI-led modernization, but the appetite has not been quantified or validated with the Economic Buyer or CFO. No evidence of AI resistance. The deal is well-positioned for the Merlin/I2O narrative if we can escalate it to power and tie it to a quantified business case.

items.change: NextStep updated: Paul liked Merlin, will reply in one week on project status
Paul's one-week commitment expired with no reply (now 7 days overdue)

deal_movement.summary: Deal created 29 Jan 2026, entered Qualified stage (date unclear from field history), last modified 5 Jun 2026. Last buyer engagement 12 Jun 2026 via NextStep: Paul confirmed Merlin resonated, no centralized system exists today, and committed to reply in one week on project status. That one-week window expired approximately 19 Jun; no reply received as of 26 Jun (7 days overdue). No stage progression, no demo scheduled, no RFP activity, no new contacts added. The deal is live but thinning—Paul's silence after a verbal commitment signals either internal delay or cooling interest.

items.name: Paul Butts
items.risk: Developing champion at risk of going passive; no introductions to power, no multi-threading, and now silent for 14 days after verbal one-week follow-up.
items.title: Director, Corporate Procurement Business Transformation
items.sentiment: Positive toward Merlin and its use cases, but engagement cooling after 7-day-overdue reply commitment.

items.name: Kathryn Schneider
items.risk: Contact role on opp but no evidence of involvement in calls, tasks, or NextStep updates.
items.title: Strategic Sourcing Manager

items.detail: Economic Buyer unmapped, no exec sponsorship secured, and no validation that the $110k Merlin investment has budget approval or CFO sign-off. Single-threaded through Paul (Director level), who is cooling after a missed follow-up commitment.

119 days to close (23 Oct) from Qualified stage with no demo, no RFP, no shortlisting, no pricing, and no contracting steps on the calendar. The October date is a placeholder unsupported by milestone velocity; expect slip unless the deal accelerates immediately.
Single-point-of-contact risk: only Paul Butts engaged, no multi-threading to Kathryn Schneider or other buyer-side stakeholders. If Paul leaves, ghosts, or loses internal priority, the deal dies.

Paul went silent after committing to a one-week reply (expected approximately 19 Jun, now 7 days overdue). This signals either internal delay (project on hold, budget review, committee discussion) or cooling interest. Without visibility into the internal political landscape, we cannot diagnose or de-risk the stall.

Competitive landscape unknown. No insight into whether Quest is running a sole-source evaluation (favoring Zycus) or a multi-vendor bake-off (requiring differentiation and urgency). Without competitive intel, we cannot tailor positioning or forecast win probability.

champion_strength.source: OpportunityContactRole, NextStep 12 Jun 2026, LastActivityDate 12 Jun (14 days ago, no reply after commitment)
champion_strength.summary: Paul Butts is a developing champion with positive sentiment toward Merlin but incomplete advocacy and cooling engagement. He expressed interest in Merlin's AI use cases on 12 Jun and committed to a one-week follow-up, but has not replied (now 7 days overdue as of 26 Jun). He has not introduced us to the Economic Buyer, Decision Maker, or other committee members, has not shared internal evaluation documents, and has not driven the agenda forward. His access to power is unconfirmed. The champion is developing but at risk of going passive if we do not re-engage with value-add content and request active advocacy (introductions, internal sell) in the next conversation.

items.action: Re-engage Paul with a Merlin ROI one-pager and request the Economic Buyer introduction.
items.trigger: Paul's one-week reply commitment expired approximately 19 Jun; no response as of 26 Jun (7 days overdue).
items.expected_effect: Restarts the conversation with value-add content (ROI model anchored to Quest's decentralized-intake pain), reconfirms Paul's internal timeline and project status, and secures an introduction to the budget authority to validate the $110k investment and October close date. Breaking single-threading and mapping the EB are the highest-leverage moves to de-risk the forecast and accelerate from Qualified.

items.action: Ask Paul who else Quest is evaluating and map the competitive landscape.
items.trigger: Competitors__c null, no competitive intel in NextStep or calls; unknown if sole-source or multi-vendor evaluation.
items.expected_effect: Reveals the competitive set (peer SaaS vendors, incumbent tools, or do-nothing/internal delay) so we can tailor Merlin's AI differentiation, adjust win strategy, and forecast win probability. If sole-source, we emphasize speed-to-value; if competitive, we position Merlin's AI intake automation and I2O agentic capabilities as the category leader.

items.action: Multi-thread to Kathryn Schneider and request introductions to finance and IT stakeholders.
items.trigger: Only 2 buyer contact roles on opp, only Paul engaged; no EB, DM, finance, IT, or InfoSec contacts mapped.
items.expected_effect: Reduces single-point-of-contact risk by engaging Kathryn (Strategic Sourcing Manager, existing contact role but no documented involvement) and securing introductions to the Economic Buyer (finance exec), IT (ERP integration owner), and InfoSec (SaaS security review). Multi-threading builds redundancy, accelerates discovery, and validates budget/timeline at the power level.

items.action: Run a discovery session with Paul and a finance or ops stakeholder to quantify the business case.
items.trigger: No quantified pain, ROI, or savings documented; NextStep 12 Jun notes lack of centralized system but no cost/impact articulated.
items.expected_effect: Builds a quantified ROI model by calculating the current-state cost of decentralized intake (manual hours, headcount, cycle time, error rate, maverick spend) and modeling Merlin's savings (automation, faster cycle time, supplier intelligence). This anchors the Economic Buyer conversation, justifies the $110k investment, and creates a compelling event tied to measurable pain.

items.action: Co-create a mutual close plan with Paul tied to the October 23 close date.
items.trigger: 119 days to close from Qualified with no demo, RFP, pricing, or contracting milestones on the calendar; October date is a placeholder.
items.expected_effect: Maps the buyer's internal timeline (RFP/RFI gates, vendor shortlisting, budget approval, InfoSec review, legal/contracting lead times) against the October 23 close date, identifies gaps or slippage risk, and builds a shared milestone plan with dated commitments from both sides. This forces visibility into their decision process, validates the close date, and creates accountability to accelerate from Qualified.

items: Paul's one-week follow-up commitment expired approximately 19 Jun; no reply as of 26 Jun (7 days overdue).
No multi-threading: only Paul engaged, no introductions to Kathryn Schneider or other stakeholders.
No demo, RFP, or milestone activity since opportunity creation 29 Jan; 148 days old, still in Qualified.
confidence_signals.summary: Paul's engagement is cooling. He committed on 12 Jun to reply in one week on the project status (expected approximately 19 Jun) but has not responded as of 26 Jun (7 days overdue). No new contacts added, no demo scheduled, no internal champion introductions, and no forward movement since 12 Jun. The deal is live (LastActivityDate 12 Jun, 14 days ago) but thinning—Paul's silence after a verbal commitment suggests either internal delay or waning priority.

north_star_verdict.math: Created 29 Jan 2026, 148 days old. Qualified stage, 119 days to close (23 Oct 2026). Last buyer engagement 12 Jun via NextStep (Paul's verbal 'one week' check-in commitment, expected reply by approximately 19 Jun, now 7 days overdue as of 26 Jun). No field-history stage progression, no submission-date milestones recorded. A Qualified deal 119 days from close with no demos, no RFI/RFP, no shortlisting, and a single-threaded buyer contact now silent for 14 days after a missed follow-up is off-pace and thinning.
evidence: NextStep 12 Jun: Paul liked Merlin and use cases, no centralized system today, will reply in one week on project status.
Paul's one-week commitment expired approximately 19 Jun; no reply received as of 26 Jun (7 days overdue).
Only 2 buyer contact roles (Paul Butts, Kathryn Schneider); no Economic Buyer, Decision Maker, or Champion formally identified.
No Avoma calls discovered via opp/account/attendee pulls; discovery depth and competitive landscape unconfirmed.
No MEDDPICC fields populated; no competitor, pain, metrics, decision process, or champion documented in Salesforce.
Products scoped (Merlin, Merlin Intake) but no pricing, no demo, no RFI/RFP activity recorded.

119 days to close from Qualified requires demo, RFP positioning, shortlisting, pricing, and contracting to compress into an aggressive timeline; current pace does not support the close date without immediate re-engagement and multi-threading.
north_star_verdict.headline: EARLY-stage deal now 14 days silent after Paul's one-week reply commitment expired (overdue since ~19 June). Single-threaded, no EB/champion mapped, no demo scheduled, and 119 days to compress discovery-to-close requires immediate re-engagement and multi-threading to salvage October timeline.

best_practice_check.summary: Three win-strategy levers to raise probability in this early-stage, single-threaded, silent deal.

competitive_position.summary: Competition is unknown. No competitors named in Salesforce, no Avoma calls discovered, and NextStep (12 Jun) does not mention any vendor evaluation or shortlist. Paul stated Quest has no centralized system today, which could indicate a greenfield opportunity (no incumbent, Zycus positioned as sole-source) or could mean they are evaluating multiple vendors to solve the centralization problem but have not disclosed the shortlist to us. The single strongest current threat is the 'do nothing' / internal delay scenario: if Quest's project is on hold or budget is frozen, the October close date slips indefinitely. Without competitive intel, we cannot assess peer threats (e.g. Coupa, SAP Ariba, Jaggaer in intake/sourcing) or tailor differentiation. The immediate move is to ask Paul directly who else they are considering and how they are solving intake today, then position Merlin's AI differentiation once we know the competitive set.

competitors.name: Do Nothing / Internal Delay
competitors.quote: Paul will get back in a week about the status of the project (12 Jun); no reply received as of 26 Jun, 7 days overdue.
competitors.how_we_win: Re-engage Paul with a quantified ROI case (cost of decentralization vs Merlin savings), secure a champion introduction to the Economic Buyer to validate budget and timeline, and co-create a mutual close plan with milestones tied to their October forcing function (if one exists). Emphasize Merlin's AI-driven intake automation as a fast-time-to-value differentiator to break internal inertia.

ai_positioning_strength.score: Strong at influencer level, unvalidated at power level
ai_positioning_strength.summary: Merlin positioning landed well with Paul (NextStep 12 Jun: 'He liked Merlin and it's use cases'), indicating strong early receptivity to AI-driven intake automation. Quest has no centralized system today, creating a greenfield opportunity for Merlin Intake to deliver fast ROI via AI orchestration. However, the AI narrative has not been socialized beyond Paul; no EB, finance, or ops stakeholder has validated the AI appetite or ROI case, and no AIS fields are populated. The positioning is strong at the influencer level but unvalidated at the power level. The next move is to package the Merlin AI value prop (intake automation, supplier intelligence, cycle-time reduction) into a quantified ROI model and present it to the Economic Buyer and CFO to secure exec sponsorship.

items.quote: He liked Merlin and it's use cases, they do not have any centralized system at the moment.
items.criterion: AI-driven intake automation to replace decentralized manual processes

customer_expectations_fit.summary: Limited insight into customer expectations; only one signal captured. Paul expressed positive sentiment toward Merlin and its use cases (NextStep 12 Jun), indicating alignment on AI-driven intake automation as a solution to their lack of a centralized system. However, no formal evaluation criteria, BRD, or RFP requirements have been shared, so fit against their weighted priorities (e.g. AI/ML depth, ERP integration, cost, implementation speed, InfoSec) is unconfirmed. The single known expectation—solving decentralized intake pain—is aligned with Merlin Intake's core value prop, but deeper validation is required.

value.flag: Discover the competitive landscape and decision process in the re-engagement call by asking Paul directly: 'Who else are you evaluating for intake automation?' and 'Walk me through your vendor selection process and internal timeline.' Without this intel, we cannot tailor differentiation, forecast win probability, or plan milestones against the October close date.

value.flag: Build a quantified business case early by running a discovery session with Paul and ideally a finance or ops stakeholder to calculate the current-state cost of decentralized intake (hours, headcount, cycle time, error rate) and model Merlin's ROI. This will anchor the EB conversation and justify the $110k investment against a measurable pain.
